segments.role: Builder or reviewer evaluating this repo
current_knowledge: Knows AI can generate slide-shaped output.
May not trust AI-generated evidence or slide quality.
Wants to know what is real today.
stakes: Needs a workflow that can survive long-running agent work.
Needs clarity about current versus future capabilities.
Needs enough visual polish to show the idea to others.
success_criteria: Can identify the current QA gates.
Can decide whether to test the repo.

likely_questions.question: Is this really agentic or just a renderer?
How does it prevent fake claims?
Can this become PowerPoint or Google Slides?

objections.objection: This may be promising but overstated.
A passing renderer does not guarantee a great-looking deck.

audience_pov_critique.what_they_care_about: Whether the repo can produce a deck they would trust and show.
audience_pov_critique.what_will_feel_unproven: Any claim that sounds like native PPTX/Google Slides generation or full autonomy.
audience_pov_critique.where_deck_sounds_like_us_not_them: Long descriptions of internal files without explaining why they matter to reviewers.
audience_pov_critique.required_reframe: Connect each artifact to a reviewer benefit: accuracy, clarity, visual QA, or repairability.
